retry_reason: The only failure condition is when ioc->firmware_event_thread is NULL, indicating the thread is gone; retrying will not revive it.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to requeue a firmware event for later processing, not solely reference-count management. It increments the refcount (fw_event_work_get) as a necessary step to prevent use-after-free while the work is pending, but also performs significant non-refcount logic: checking thread existence, adding to a linked list, initializing delayed work, and queuing it. This is not a pure get/set wrapper because the refcount operation is subordinate to the requeue operation.
judge.retry_reason: The only failure condition is when ioc->firmware_event_thread is NULL, indicating the thread is gone; retrying will not revive it.

final.retry_reason: The only failure condition is when ioc->firmware_event_thread is NULL, indicating the thread is gone; retrying will not revive it.
